description: An ultra-short-acting opioid painkiller given by IV infusion during anaesthesia. Uniquely among opioids it is broken down by blood and tissue esterases, so its effect fades within minutes of stopping the infusion.

halfLife.conditions: terminal (γ-phase) eigenvalue half-life = ln2/γ = 51.0 min, computed by the engine from the disposition3c micro-parameters (γ = 0.815/h; carried here for the caption/provenance rows, and the engine round-trips to it). IMPORTANT — do not read this as 'the' elimination half-life: the terminal γ mode carries only ~0.09% of the initial concentration (the IV-bolus disposition splits 88.9% α + 11.0% β + 0.09% γ by amplitude, because γ = 0.815/h nearly coincides with the deep back-rate k31 = 0.841/h). So 51 min is a very-low-amplitude, near-sub-assay deep-compartment tail, NOT the observable terminal decline (that is the β phase, ~6.5 min) and NOT what governs clinical offset (the ~3 min context-sensitive half-time). See notes.

vd.conditions: steady-state volume of distribution Vd(ss) = V1 + V2 + V3 = 5.1 + 9.82 + 5.42 = 20.34 L (the sum of the three Minto compartment volumes, which IS Vss for a mammillary model), for the reference individual — carried for the caption/provenance rows. Reported whole-blood Vd(ss) for remifentanil is often quoted higher (~30 L); that discrepancy is a between-study/assay difference and does NOT change the plotted curve, whose shape is set by the six disposition3c parameters (C(0) = Dose/V1). Already absolute litres (no per-kg scaling).

clearance.conditions: central (metabolic) clearance CL (Cl1) for the Minto reference individual (age 40, lean body mass 55 kg) — 2.6 L/min = 156 L/h. High extrahepatic clearance by non-specific blood/tissue esterases; sets AUC = Dose/CL.

centralVd.conditions: central volume V1 for the Minto reference individual (age 40, LBM 55 kg) — the concentration reference C(0) = Dose/V1. Read directly from the Minto 1997 covariate model at its centred reference point (the equation intercept); no offline derivation.

interCompartmentalClearance2.conditions: inter-compartmental clearance Q2 (Cl2) to the rapidly-equilibrating peripheral compartment, Minto reference individual — 2.05 L/min = 123 L/h. Drives the fast α distribution phase.

peripheralVd2.conditions: rapidly-equilibrating peripheral volume V2, Minto reference individual (age 40, LBM 55 kg).

interCompartmentalClearance3.conditions: inter-compartmental clearance Q3 (Cl3) to the slow (deep) peripheral compartment, Minto reference individual — 0.076 L/min = 4.56 L/h. The small Q3 and its slow deep volume govern the long terminal γ phase.

peripheralVd3.conditions: slow (deep) peripheral volume V3 — fixed at 5.42 L in the Minto model (no covariate dependence), so it is identical for every reference individual.

minto_1997.title: Minto CF, Schnider TW, Egan TD, et al. Influence of age and gender on the pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of remifentanil. I. Model development. Anesthesiology. 1997;86(1):10–23 (three-compartment mammillary model; V1/V2/V3, Cl1/Cl2/Cl3 as covariate functions of age and lean body mass; reference values are the equation intercepts at age 40, LBM 55 kg)

fda_label.title: ULTIVA (remifentanil hydrochloride) for injection — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology (linear/dose-proportional pharmacokinetics; rapid metabolism by non-specific blood and tissue esterases to an essentially inactive acid metabolite; blood-brain equilibration ~1 min; context-sensitive half-time ~3–5 min independent of infusion duration)

notes:
FIRST real three-compartment compound (handoff §12, Stage B), and the first to exercise the absolute-litre / L-per-min disposition units (every prior compound used per-kg forms). Remifentanil is a µ-opioid agonist given by IV infusion in anaesthesia; it is modelled here purely to illustrate a tri-phasic (α/β/γ) disposition curve. EDUCATIONAL ILLUSTRATION ONLY — not a dosing or clinical tool.

WHY REMIFENTANIL. It is the textbook clean case: (1) genuinely LINEAR — the FDA label and Minto 1997 both report dose-proportional exposure with dose-independent clearance and Vss, because elimination is by high-capacity non-specific esterases (no saturable enzyme, no autoinhibition); (2) directly PARAMETERISED as a three-compartment model — Minto 1997 reports V1/V2/V3 and Cl1/Cl2/Cl3 outright, so unlike diazepam (whose Q/Vp had to be back-computed offline) NOTHING here is derived — all six disposition3c values are read straight from the model; (3) IV-only, so there is no oral absorption to source (and oral 3-comp derivation is deferred anyway).

REFERENCE INDIVIDUAL = THE ILLUSTRATIVE SUBJECT. The Minto parameters are covariate functions of age and lean body mass. Rather than pick a specific person (whose LBM depends on height and the choice of LBM formula), this file uses the model's OWN centred reference point — age 40, LBM 55 kg — at which each covariate equation collapses to its intercept: V1=5.1 L, V2=9.82 L, V3=5.42 L (fixed), Cl1=2.6, Cl2=2.05, Cl3=0.076 L/min. These intercepts are the canonical, unambiguous 'Minto reference' values and are directly citable. LBM 55 kg is consistent with a ~70 kg adult, so we adopt the Minto reference individual AS the project's 70 kg illustrative subject for this compound (our subject is otherwise specified only by weight; Minto needs a fuller covariate point, and this is the honest bridge). No per-kg scaling is applied — the values are already absolute litres / L·min⁻¹ for that individual.

THE THREE HALF-LIVES, BY AMPLITUDE — the teachable moment. The engine computes three eigenvalues: α t½ ≈ 0.67 min (distribution), β t½ ≈ 6.5 min (intermediate), γ t½ ≈ 51 min (terminal). But their AMPLITUDES are wildly unequal: for an IV bolus the initial concentration splits 88.9% into the α mode, 11.0% into β, and just 0.09% into the terminal γ mode (γ = 0.815/h sits almost exactly on the deep back-rate k31 = 0.841/h, so its residue nearly vanishes). Three distinct 'half-lives' coexist and NONE alone describes the drug: (a) the terminal γ (~51 min) is a real eigenvalue but a sub-0.1%, near-sub-assay deep-compartment tail — NOT 'the' terminal half-life a study would report; (b) the β phase (~6.5 min) carries essentially all of the observable terminal decline and is the region the commonly-quoted 'terminal half-life ~10–20 min' figures track; (c) neither governs clinical offset — that is the ~3 min CONTEXT-SENSITIVE half-time, a whole-system property no single eigenvalue captures. disposition.halfLife carries the engine's terminal γ (51 min) because that is definitionally the terminal (slowest) eigenvalue, but the whole point of shipping remifentanil is that reading it as 'the half-life' misleads three different ways at once — this project's epistemic-honesty thesis, now visible in the α/β/γ caption. (The 88.9/11.0/0.09% amplitude split is computed from the stored parameters, not cited.)

Vd(ss). disposition.vd is the sum V1+V2+V3 = 20.34 L (= Vss for a mammillary model), carried only for the caption/provenance rows. Reported blood Vd(ss) is sometimes quoted nearer ~30 L (assay/population differences); that does not affect the plotted curve, whose shape is fixed by the six disposition3c parameters and whose initial concentration is Dose/V1.

ROUTES. iv_infusion is marked available — continuous infusion (and TCI) is remifentanil's real clinical route and the setting the Minto model was built for. iv_bolus is offered as inferred (available:false): a rapid bolus is not standard (it risks opioid-induced muscle/chest-wall rigidity) and is shown only to illustrate the instantaneous-input curve shape. Oral is not offered (remifentanil is not given orally, and oral 3-comp derivation is deferred).

DOSE SCALE. Clinical remifentanil doses are in MICROGRAMS (a bolus is tens of µg; infusions are µg·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹). The plotter's generic milligram dose field and the linear curve still illustrate the correct SHAPE and the α/β/γ structure; the concentration-unit toggle (µg/mL, ng/mL) puts the y-axis in familiar territory. The absolute numbers are illustrative, per the standing disclaimer.
